title: psilotrichopsis cochinchinensis
intro: Psilotrichopsis cochinchinensis, also known as Vietnamese coriander or rau ram, is a type of herb belonging to the Psilotrichaceae family. It is traditionally used in Southeast Asian cuisine, particularly in Vietnamese and Thai dishes, for its unique flavor and aroma. The medicinal properties of Psilotrichopsis cochinchinensis include its ability to treat fever, diarrhea, and respiratory issues due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of consuming this herb include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and providing antioxidant properties, which can be achieved through various herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and dried leaves.
